provenance.source_corpus: Eight-year ecophysiology and growth dynamics of Picea rubens seedlings planted in harvest gaps of partially cut stands

nodes.name: hybrid single-tree and group-selection cutting treatments

source_spans.text: The experimental design compared four cutting treatments: an uncut control (26 m2·ha−1 of merchantable basal area [BA], with mean natural canopy gap areas of ≤ 100 m2 each) and three hybrid single-tree and group-selection cuttings corresponding to a light cut (20% removal; residual BA [RBA]: 21 m2·ha−1; mean harvest gap area: 220 m2), a moderate cut (31% removal; RBA: 18 m2·ha−1; gap area: 270 m2), and a heavy cut (42% removal; RBA: 15 m2·ha−1; gap area: 340 m2).
Morphophysiological performance of seedlings was best in the heavy cut creating the largest gaps.
Three years later, a mechanical release treatment was applied in half of planted plots to limit competing vegetation.
After eight years, seedling survival in the three cuts was equally high (>80%), and greater than in the control (40%).
Morphophysiological performance of seedlings was best in the heavy cut creating the largest gaps.
Initial (2009) summer light availability was significantly different among cutting treatments.
Released seedlings had significantly greater Amax only in the heavy cut (+27%, P = 0.005; Fig. 2d).
Released seedlings had a greater WUE in cuts compared to the control (+20%, P < 0.001; Fig. 2f).
Release reduced SLA to a similar extent in all cuts (−9%, P < 0.001; Fig. 2h).
Generally, HG and DG were greater in cuts (9.5 cm·year−1 and 1.9 mm·year−1, respectively) than in the control (2.5 cm·year−1 and 0.4 mm·year−1,P < 0.001;Table 4,Figs. 3a, c and4a–d).
Generally, HG and DG were greater in cuts (9.5 cm·year−1 and 1.9 mm·year−1, respectively) than in the control (2.5 cm·year−1 and 0.4 mm·year−1,P < 0.001;Table 4,Figs. 3a, c and4a–d).

nodes.name: number of branches composing the annual whorl (NBW)

source_spans.text: Overall, released seedlings also had greater NBW (+29%,P < 0.001; Fig. 4h).
After release, NIB was affected by the C × R interaction (P= 0.028), because release enhanced NIB only in cuts (+85%,P = 0.047;Fig. 4j).

edges.original_sentence: The % of total transmitted light varied according to a consistent gradient (heavy cut > moderate cut > light cut > uncut control), with mean values of 33.2%, 19.8%, 17.6%, and 13.3% of full light at 1.3 m, respectively (P= 0.002;Raymond et al., 2018).

source_spans.text: The % of total transmitted light varied according to a consistent gradient (heavy cut > moderate cut > light cut > uncut control), with mean values of 33.2%, 19.8%, 17.6%, and 13.3% of full light at 1.3 m, respectively (P= 0.002;Raymond et al., 2018).
